expectations.text: Reads or references scene-briefs.csv as the primary data source
expectations.evidence: Explicitly analyzes key_actions, key_dialogue, emotions fields from scene-briefs.csv with specific values quoted

expectations.text: Mentions abstract language, abstract indicators, or thematic vs concrete language
expectations.evidence: References abstract language plus 4 additional quality dimensions: over-specified beats, prescriptive dialogue, procedural goals, emotional arc granularity

expectations.text: Names at least one specific scene ID from the project data
expectations.evidence: Names act1-sc01, act1-sc02, act2-sc03, new-x1, act2-sc01, act2-sc02 with per-scene analysis

expectations.text: Recommends the briefs domain or concretization as the action
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'The briefs domain of hone is exactly what you need. It will concretize abstract language, trim over-specified beats, convert prescriptive dialogue to direction, and simplify emotional arcs.'

expectations.text: Offers script delegation with Option A (run here) and Option B (run yourself) pattern
expectations.evidence: Provides explicit Option A / Option B blocks with full bash commands including --domain briefs and --scenes variants
